title: AI Agents and the Unseen Work of War
gist: Podcast episode notes (Cogs of War teaser) featuring Bill Pessin, Salesforce SVP of national security and former Army logistics officer, on how military organizations can use AI agents for coordination, administration, and logistics, what makes these tools different from ordinary software, and why safety and accountability matter.

insights.claim: Most military effort is not at the front but in coordination, administration, logistics, and judgment, the 'unseen work of war' where AI agents could be applied.
insights.speaker: Bill Pessin and Jonathan Panter
insights.so_what: Points to military logistics/admin as the under-discussed surface area for enterprise AI-agent adoption.
insights.quote: Behind every operation is a vast amount of coordination, administration, logistics, and judgment.
insights.locator: Armies run on more than what happens at the front.

insights.claim: The discussion centers on what makes AI agents different from ordinary software and why safety and accountability matter when the technology enters national security work.
insights.speaker: Bill Pessin and Jonathan Panter
insights.so_what: Flags accountability/safety as the gating factor for AI-agent procurement in defense, relevant to vendors like Salesforce.
insights.quote: why safety and accountability matter when new technology enters national security work.
